---
title: "Register-based research at DST"
subtitle: "Who is this guide for, and what will you learn?"
date: last-modified
---

This guide is for anyone working with register-based research at Statistics Denmark (DST).
No prior coding experience required.

---

## Where do you start?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4}
### Completely new?
Follow the phases from the beginning.

→ [1 — Plan your study](01_studieforberedelse.qmd)\
→ [2 — R: the bare essentials](02_r-intro.qmd)\
→ [3 — Log in to DST](03_log-ind-dst.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Know R, new to DST?
Skip phase 2 and start with the server.

→ [3 — Log in to DST](03_log-ind-dst.qmd)\
→ [4 — File types and loading](04_filformater.qmd)\
→ [5 — The universal pattern](05_universelle-moenster.qmd)\
→ [8 — Know your registers](08_kend-dine-registre.qmd)\
→ [14 — Reference](14_reference.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Working on DARTER?
Project-specific setup steps and guidance.

→ [DARTER — overview and pipeline](darter/00_index.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.callout-tip}
**Looking for something specific?** Use the search box in the top right — it searches across the entire guide.
:::

---

## SDS and DST

As a researcher, you work on **Statistics Denmark's (DST) servers**.
DST receives and processes data from, among others, the Danish Health Data Authority (SDS), which holds the raw national health registers (LPR, LMDB, cancer register etc.) and makes them available to researchers via a secure remote connection.

---

## From research question to analysis-ready dataset

Register-based research typically follows these steps — in this order. The terms *cohort*, *index date*, *exposure* and *outcome* are explained in [Phase 1](01_studieforberedelse.qmd#key-concepts).

1. **Formulate a precise research question** — who, what, when, compared with whom → [Phase 1](01_studieforberedelse.qmd)
2. **Map your data model** — which registers cover exposure, outcome and covariates? → [Phase 1](01_studieforberedelse.qmd) · [Phase 8](08_kend-dine-registre.qmd)
3. **Identify your study population** — who is included, when do they enter, when are they censored? → [Phase 10](10_byg-din-kohorte.qmd)
4. **Extract exposure** — retrieve the event defining your groups (e.g. surgery, diagnosis, prescription) → [Phase 9](09_hospitalskontakter-lpr.qmd) · [Phase 10](10_byg-din-kohorte.qmd)
5. **Extract outcome** — retrieve the event you want to measure (e.g. dementia onset, hospitalisation, death) → [Phase 9](09_hospitalskontakter-lpr.qmd)
6. **Extract covariates** — demographic, clinical and socioeconomic variables → [Phase 6](06_foerste-udtraek.qmd) · [Phase 12](12_socioekonomiske-variable.qmd)
7. **Assemble the dataset** — link the separate extracts with joins, handle missing data → [Phase 11](11_data-manipulation.qmd)
8. **Analyse and export** — run your statistical model, review output, export results → [Phase 13](13a_pakker-oversigt.qmd) · [Phase 15](15_eksport-hjemsendelse.qmd)
